title: Economic development
paperName: Cambridge International A Level Economics (P3 & P4)
summary: 9708 A Level — development indicators, human capital, and sustainable growth.
learningObjectives: Define economic development vs economic growth.
Use HDI and other development indicators.
Explain role of education, health, and infrastructure.

simpleExplanation.title: More than GDP
simpleExplanation.summary: Development includes living standards, literacy, life expectancy, and institutional quality — not just output growth.

flashcards.back: Growth = rise in real GDP/output. Development = sustained improvement in living standards, welfare, and capabilities — multidimensional.
Human Development Index — composite of GNI per capita, mean years of schooling, expected years of schooling, and life expectancy.
Investment in education and health raises worker productivity → shifts LRAS right → potential growth.
Property rights, rule of law, low corruption — encourage investment and entrepreneurship, supporting development.
Meeting present needs without compromising future generations — balances economic, social, and environmental goals.
Gini (inequality), literacy rate, infant mortality, access to clean water, MPI (multidimensional poverty index).

sections.content: **Economic development** (11.3) uses **11.4–11.6** case material. Cite **specific indicators**, not vague "poverty" — HDI components, Gini, sector shares.

items: **GDP per capita** alone is insufficient — ignores distribution and non-market activity.
**Human capital:** education and health are both consumption and investment.
**Infrastructure:** transport, power, telecoms reduce costs, attract FDI.

sections.question:
Country B's real GDP grows at 6% per year but its HDI rank falls from 145 to 152. Life expectancy is stagnant at 58 years, literacy is 52%, and 40% of the population lacks clean water access.

Explain why GDP growth may not indicate development and evaluate two policies to promote sustainable development. [12 marks]
sections.solution:
**Why GDP growth ≠ development:**
- **HDI falling** despite 6% GDP growth → gains not reaching **health, education, or income per capita** broadly.
- **Stagnant life expectancy (58)** → healthcare, nutrition, sanitation failing — GDP may reflect **extractive industries** (mining) with limited spillovers.
- **Low literacy (52%)** → human capital not building — limits future productivity.
- **Clean water access (60%)** → basic welfare unmet — **multidimensional poverty** persists.
- **Possible causes:** inequality (GDP concentrated), **environmental degradation**, **informal economy** excluded from data but households suffer.

**Policy 1 — Human capital investment (education + health):**
- Free primary education, teacher training → **literacy rises** → productivity, LRAS shifts right.
- Clean water and vaccination programmes → **life expectancy rises** → direct HDI improvement.
- *Evaluation:* fiscal cost high; benefits **long-term**; requires governance to prevent corruption.

**Policy 2 — Sustainable infrastructure:**
- Renewable energy, rural water systems → raises productivity **and** reduces environmental damage.
- *Evaluation:* may need **foreign aid or FDI**; avoids resource-depleting growth path.

**Judgement:** **6% GDP growth is misleading** — development requires **targeted social investment** and **sustainable practices**, not aggregate output alone.

steps.caption: Development: sustained ↑ living standards, HDI, not just GDP.
Human capital: education and health raise productivity.
Sustainable development balances growth and environment.
